questions.question: How many 32-bit floats can fit in a single AVX (256-bit) vector register?
questions.explanation: AVX registers are 256 bits wide. Each float32 is 32 bits, so 256 / 32 = 8 floats fit in one AVX register.

questions.question: Which of these loop patterns is most likely to be auto-vectorized by the compiler?
options: for (i=0; i<n; i++) a[i] = b[i] * c[i];
for (i=0; i<n; i++) a[i] = rand();
for (i=0; i<n; i++) if (a[i] > 0) break;
questions.explanation: The second loop has no loop-carried dependencies, no function calls, and simple control flow — all conditions for auto-vectorization. The first has a dependency, the third calls rand(), and the fourth has a break.

questions.question: What does the __restrict__ keyword do in C/C++ that helps auto-vectorization?
options: It forces the compiler to use SIMD instructions
It tells the compiler the pointed-to memory doesn't overlap with other restrict pointers
It aligns the pointer to a 32-byte boundary
It prevents the compiler from unrolling the loop
questions.explanation: __restrict__ (or restrict in C) is a promise that the pointer doesn't alias other restrict-qualified pointers. Without it, the compiler must assume pointers may overlap, blocking vectorization.

questions.question: What is the difference between _mm_load_ps and _mm_loadu_ps in SSE intrinsics?
options: _mm_load_ps loads 8 floats; _mm_loadu_ps loads 4
_mm_load_ps requires 16-byte alignment; _mm_loadu_ps works with any alignment
_mm_load_ps is for integers; _mm_loadu_ps is for floats
There is no difference; they are aliases
questions.explanation: _mm_load_ps (movaps) requires the address to be 16-byte aligned and faults if not. _mm_loadu_ps (movups) works at any alignment. On modern CPUs, the performance difference is minimal, but aligned loads are semantically stricter.

questions.question: AVX-512 added 8 mask registers (k0–k7). What is their primary purpose?
options: They store loop counters for vectorized loops
They hold per-lane predicates for conditional SIMD execution without branches
They replace the zmm registers for integer operations
They manage cache line prefetching
questions.explanation: AVX-512 mask registers hold per-lane boolean values (one bit per SIMD lane). They enable masked operations like _mm512_mask_add_ps that only operate on selected lanes, eliminating the need for scalar fallback code.

questions.question: You benchmark a dot product and find that the auto-vectorized version (compiled with -O3) runs just as fast as your hand-written AVX intrinsics version. What is the most likely explanation?
options: Your intrinsics code has a bug and isn't actually using SIMD
The compiler auto-vectorizer is good enough for this simple pattern — intrinsics don't always beat it
AVX intrinsics are always slower than scalar code
The benchmark is wrong because -O3 disables SIMD
questions.explanation: For simple patterns like dot product (no gather/scatter, no masking, just multiply-accumulate), modern auto-vectorizers generate code equivalent to hand-written intrinsics. Intrinsics provide value when the compiler can't prove vectorization is safe, or when you need gather/scatter, masked ops, or specific instruction sequences.
